name: Build an iOS App
description: A native iOS application using SwiftUI

universal.note: These are installed during onboarding, not per-blueprint

blueprint.name: Xcode
blueprint.blockerMessage: iOS development requires Xcode, which can only be installed from the App Store.

blueprint.name: iOS Simulator
blueprint.note: Downloaded automatically if Xcode is installed but simulator runtimes are missing

blockers.name: Xcode
blockers.resolution: Install Xcode from the App Store, then relaunch TWEEBS

authRequirements.name: Apple Developer Account
authRequirements.note: Only needed for deploying to the App Store or testing on a physical device. Simulator testing works without it.

postScaffold: Create Xcode project structure with SwiftUI App lifecycle
scaffolding.note: Xcode project creation may be handled by the mobile-engineer Tweeb directly since swift package init doesn't create a full iOS app structure. The Tweeb will use xcodebuild or create the .xcodeproj manually.

initialContext: The user wants to build an iOS app. Ask what the app does (one sentence), who it's for, and the 2-3 most important screens. Don't ask about technical details — the architect will handle those. Then hand off to architect for app architecture, designer for UI/UX, and mobile engineer for implementation.

ticketTemplate.title: UI design system and screen mockups
Build core screens
dependsOn: UI design system and screen mockups
